name: Ken's Fresh Fish
tagline: Takeout-only, tiny, and almost impossible to find — the ahi katsu here is possibly the single best dish on the entire North Shore.
mustOrder: Ahi katsu — panko-crusted seared ahi, rare inside, possibly the best thing on the North Shore
Fresh poke — made daily from locally caught fish
Marlin tacos — unique preparation you won't find anywhere else
parking: Small gravel lot next to the shop
talkStory: Ken Higa is a working fisherman in Laie who started selling his daily catch directly to the community from a tiny takeout window — word spread through the North Shore surf network and now people drive from town for his ahi katsu. The panko-crusted seared ahi (rare inside) is arguably the single best prepared fish dish on the North Shore. Menu changes daily based on what Ken caught that morning. Open only Tue/Wed/Fri/Sat with limited hours — call ahead to check if they're open. No seating, takeout only, and once it's gone, it's gone.
